text.content: 1.0  Opening Frame  [s1_0_opening_frame]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.0  Opening Frame  [s1_0_opening_frame]

text.content: "You read and created bar graphs with scale of 10. You figured out values at the lines AND between the lines. Let's look at some patterns you discovered."

rich_text.plain_text: "You read and created bar graphs with scale of 10. You figured out values at the lines AND between the lines. Let's look at some patterns you discovered."

text.content: 1.1  Scale Progression Pattern Type Pattern  [s1_1_scale_progression_pattern_type_pattern]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.1  Scale Progression Pattern Type Pattern  [s1_1_scale_progression_pattern_type_pattern]

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears on left. Scale of 1. Axis shows 0, 1, 2, 3... 20. Bar reaches 20. 20 axis lines visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears on left. Scale of 1. Axis shows 0, 1, 2, 3... 20. Bar reaches 20. 20 axis lines visible.

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears second from left. Scale of 2. Axis shows 0, 2, 4, 6... 20. Bar reaches 20. 10 axis lines visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears second from left. Scale of 2. Axis shows 0, 2, 4, 6... 20. Bar reaches 20. 10 axis lines visible.

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears third from left. Scale of 5. Axis shows 0, 5, 10, 15, 20. Bar reaches 20. 4 axis lines visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears third from left. Scale of 5. Axis shows 0, 5, 10, 15, 20. Bar reaches 20. 4 axis lines visible.

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears on right. Scale of 10. Axis shows 0, 10, 20. Bar reaches 20. 2 axis lines visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears on right. Scale of 10. Axis shows 0, 10, 20. Bar reaches 20. 2 axis lines visible.

text.content: "Look at all four graphs. They show the same amount: 20. Which scale uses the fewest lines to show the same amount?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at all four graphs. They show the same amount: 20. Which scale uses the fewest lines to show the same amount?"

text.content:
tool: click_category  target: bar_graph_scale_10
"Which scale uses the fewest lines to show the same amount?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: click_category  target: bar_graph_scale_10
"Which scale uses the fewest lines to show the same amount?"

text.content: ✅ Student clicked scale of 10 graph, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student clicked scale of 10 graph, correct

text.content: Scale of 10 graph highlights to confirm selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Scale of 10 graph highlights to confirm selection.

text.content: "Right. Scale of 10 uses the fewest lines. Each line shows more, so you need fewer of them."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. Scale of 10 uses the fewest lines. Each line shows more, so you need fewer of them."

text.content: ❌ Student clicked a wrong graph on first attempt

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student clicked a wrong graph on first attempt

text.content: "Look closely. Count the lines on each graph. Which one has the fewest?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look closely. Count the lines on each graph. Which one has the fewest?"

text.content: ❌ Student clicked a wrong graph on second attempt

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student clicked a wrong graph on second attempt

text.content: Scale of 10 graph highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Scale of 10 graph highlights.

text.content: "Let me help. The graph on the right shows only 2 lines: 0, 10, 20. Count the lines on the others. Which has the fewest?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me help. The graph on the right shows only 2 lines: 0, 10, 20. Count the lines on the others. Which has the fewest?"

text.content: ❌ Student clicked a wrong graph three or more times. System models the answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student clicked a wrong graph three or more times. System models the answer.

text.content: Scale of 10 graph highlights to show the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: Scale of 10 graph highlights to show the correct answer.

text.content: "Let me show you. The scale of 1 graph has 20 lines. The scale of 2 has 10 lines. The scale of 5 has 4 lines. The scale of 10 has only 2 lines. Scale of 10 uses the fewest lines because each line represents a bigger jump."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. The scale of 1 graph has 20 lines. The scale of 2 has 10 lines. The scale of 5 has 4 lines. The scale of 10 has only 2 lines. Scale of 10 uses the fewest lines because each line represents a bigger jump."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next

text.content: "You've learned four scales now: 1, 2, 5, and 10. Each one is useful for different sized numbers. Bigger numbers work better with bigger scales."

rich_text.plain_text: "You've learned four scales now: 1, 2, 5, and 10. Each one is useful for different sized numbers. Bigger numbers work better with bigger scales."

rich_text.plain_text: 2.1  Orientation Transfer Type D Representation  [s2_1_orientation_transfer_type_d_representation]

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears on left side. Scale of 10. Category B bar reaches 35, positioned between 30 and 40 gridlines.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears on left side. Scale of 10. Category B bar reaches 35, positioned between 30 and 40 gridlines.

text.content: Horizontal bar graph appears on right side. Same data, same scale of 10. Category B bar reaches 35, positioned between 30 and 40 gridlines.

rich_text.plain_text: Horizontal bar graph appears on right side. Same data, same scale of 10. Category B bar reaches 35, positioned between 30 and 40 gridlines.

text.content: "Here's the same data shown two ways, vertical and horizontal. Find the category with 35 in BOTH graphs."

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's the same data shown two ways, vertical and horizontal. Find the category with 35 in BOTH graphs."

tool: click_category  target: bar_graph_vertical
"What category shows 35 in BOTH graphs?"

tool: click_category  target: bar_graph_vertical
"What category shows 35 in BOTH graphs?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Not quite. Look for the bar that reaches between the 30 and 40 lines."

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student clicked a wrong category on second attempt

text.content: "Here's a hint: follow the gridlines to see where each bar stops. The bar showing 35 is between 30 and 40. Which category is that?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's a hint: follow the gridlines to see where each bar stops. The bar showing 35 is between 30 and 40. Which category is that?"

text.content: "Let me show you. I follow the top of each bar to see where it lands on the axis. Category A reaches 20. Category C reaches 40. Category D reaches 15. Category B reaches between 30 and 40, so that's 35. Category B is the answer."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. I follow the top of each bar to see where it lands on the axis. Category A reaches 20. Category C reaches 40. Category D reaches 15. Category B reaches between 30 and 40, so that's 35. Category B is the answer."

rich_text.plain_text: "Now find 35 in the horizontal graph."

tool: click_category  target: bar_graph_horizontal

text.content: ✅ Student clicked Category B in horizontal graph, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student clicked Category B in horizontal graph, correct

text.content: Category B bar highlights in horizontal graph.

rich_text.plain_text: Category B bar highlights in horizontal graph.

text.content: "That's it. Same value, same position between the lines, just different directions. The axis works the same way whether bars go up or sideways. The math doesn't change, only the direction."

rich_text.plain_text: "That's it. Same value, same position between the lines, just different directions. The axis works the same way whether bars go up or sideways. The math doesn't change, only the direction."

text.content: ❌ Student clicked a wrong category on first attempt

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student clicked a wrong category on first attempt

text.content: "Look at where the bar ends. Which bar reaches between the 30 and 40 lines?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at where the bar ends. Which bar reaches between the 30 and 40 lines?"

text.content: ❌ Student clicked a wrong category on second attempt

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student clicked a wrong category on second attempt

text.content: Category B bar highlights in horizontal graph.

rich_text.plain_text: Category B bar highlights in horizontal graph.

text.content: "Let's think about this together. In a horizontal graph, bars go sideways instead of up. Find the bar that reaches between 30 and 40. Which one is it?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Let's think about this together. In a horizontal graph, bars go sideways instead of up. Find the bar that reaches between 30 and 40. Which one is it?"

text.content: ❌ Student clicked a wrong category three or more times. System models the answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student clicked a wrong category three or more times. System models the answer.

text.content: Guideline draws from end of Category B bar to axis, showing it lands between 30 and 40.

rich_text.plain_text: Guideline draws from end of Category B bar to axis, showing it lands between 30 and 40.

text.content: "This one takes a bit of thought, so let's work through it together. I check where each bar ends. Category A ends at 20. Category C ends at 40. Category D ends at 15. Category B ends between 30 and 40, which is 35. The bars go sideways, but the axis still shows the same values. Category B is the answer."

rich_text.plain_text: "This one takes a bit of thought, so let's work through it together. I check where each bar ends. Category A ends at 20. Category C ends at 40. Category D ends at 15. Category B ends between 30 and 40, which is 35. The bars go sideways, but the axis still shows the same values. Category B is the answer."

text.content: 3.1  Real World Connection Type C  [s3_1_real_world_connection_type_c]

rich_text.plain_text: 3.1  Real World Connection Type C  [s3_1_real_world_connection_type_c]

text.content: Three real-world scenario images appear. A: Counting money (stacks of 10 dollar bills). B: Measuring height (ruler marked in 10s of centimeters). C: Counting items one by one.

rich_text.plain_text: Three real-world scenario images appear. A: Counting money (stacks of 10 dollar bills). B: Measuring height (ruler marked in 10s of centimeters). C: Counting items one by one.

text.content: "Scale of 10 is useful when numbers are bigger. Where might you see counting by 10s outside of math class?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Scale of 10 is useful when numbers are bigger. Where might you see counting by 10s outside of math class?"

text.content:
tool: multi_select  options: ["A", "B", "C"]
"Which situations would work well with a scale of 10? Select all that apply."

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multi_select  options: ["A", "B", "C"]
"Which situations would work well with a scale of 10? Select all that apply."

text.content: ✅ Student selected A and B, both involve counting by 10s

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected A and B, both involve counting by 10s

text.content: "Right. Money and measuring often use 10s. That's why scale of 10 feels natural for bigger amounts."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. Money and measuring often use 10s. That's why scale of 10 feels natural for bigger amounts."

text.content: ❌ Branch 2: Student selected only A (under-selecting, correct but incomplete)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Branch 2: Student selected only A (under-selecting, correct but incomplete)

text.content: Image A pulses to confirm selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Image A pulses to confirm selection.

text.content: "Money does use 10s. Look at the other images. Are there any others that group things by 10?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Money does use 10s. Look at the other images. Are there any others that group things by 10?"

text.content: ❌ Branch 2: Student selected only B (under-selecting, correct but incomplete)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Branch 2: Student selected only B (under-selecting, correct but incomplete)

text.content: Image B pulses to confirm selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Image B pulses to confirm selection.

text.content: "Measuring does use 10s. Look at the other images. Are there any others that group things by 10?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Measuring does use 10s. Look at the other images. Are there any others that group things by 10?"

text.content: ❌ Branch 3: Student selected only C or nothing (all-wrong)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Branch 3: Student selected only C or nothing (all-wrong)

text.content: Images A and B dim slightly to draw attention.

rich_text.plain_text: Images A and B dim slightly to draw attention.

text.content: "Scale of 10 means counting in groups of 10. Look at the money and the ruler. What pattern do you see on both?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Scale of 10 means counting in groups of 10. Look at the money and the ruler. What pattern do you see on both?"

text.content: ❌ Branch 4: Student selected C plus at least one correct answer (mixed correct and incorrect)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Branch 4: Student selected C plus at least one correct answer (mixed correct and incorrect)

text.content: Images A and B highlight, C dims.

rich_text.plain_text: Images A and B highlight, C dims.

text.content: "You found situations that use 10s. But counting one by one doesn't use groups of 10. Which images show counting by 10s?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You found situations that use 10s. But counting one by one doesn't use groups of 10. Which images show counting by 10s?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: Images A and B highlight. Annotation appears: '10, 20, 30...' over both.

rich_text.plain_text: Images A and B highlight. Annotation appears: '10, 20, 30...' over both.

text.content: "Let me show you. Look at image A: dollar bills in stacks of 10. Look at image B: the ruler has marks at 10, 20, 30. Both count by 10s. Image C counts one by one, not in groups of 10. The answers are A and B. When you see bigger numbers organized in groups of 10, scale of 10 makes counting faster."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. Look at image A: dollar bills in stacks of 10. Look at image B: the ruler has marks at 10, 20, 30. Both count by 10s. Image C counts one by one, not in groups of 10. The answers are A and B. When you see bigger numbers organized in groups of 10, scale of 10 makes counting faster."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next

text.content: "You already know how to count by 10s. Bar graphs use the same skill you use with money and measuring."

rich_text.plain_text: "You already know how to count by 10s. Bar graphs use the same skill you use with money and measuring."

text.content: 4.1  Metacognitive Reflection Strategy Identification  [s4_1_metacognitive_reflection_strategy_identification]

rich_text.plain_text: 4.1  Metacognitive Reflection Strategy Identification  [s4_1_metacognitive_reflection_strategy_identification]

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears. Single bar highlighted, ending at 45 between 40 and 50 gridlines. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears. Single bar highlighted, ending at 45 between 40 and 50 gridlines. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

text.content: "This bar shows 45, between the lines. How did you figure that out during the lesson?"

rich_text.plain_text: "This bar shows 45, between the lines. How did you figure that out during the lesson?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["I picked the line closest to the top of the bar", "I added the two numbers on the lines", "I found halfway—that's always 5 more", "I guessed"]
"What strategy helped you find values between the lines?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["I picked the line closest to the top of the bar", "I added the two numbers on the lines", "I found halfway—that's always 5 more", "I guessed"]
"What strategy helped you find values between the lines?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected the halfway pattern strategy

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected the halfway pattern strategy

text.content: "Right. Half of 10 is 5, so halfway between any two lines is 5 more than the lower line."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. Half of 10 is 5, so halfway between any two lines is 5 more than the lower line."

text.content: ❌ Student selected the closest-line strategy (doesn't account for between-line precision)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected the closest-line strategy (doesn't account for between-line precision)

text.content: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

rich_text.plain_text: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

text.content: "That gets you close, but the bar is exactly halfway between 40 and 50. What number is halfway?"

rich_text.plain_text: "That gets you close, but the bar is exactly halfway between 40 and 50. What number is halfway?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected adding the gridline numbers (arithmetic error—confuses sum with midpoint)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected adding the gridline numbers (arithmetic error—confuses sum with midpoint)

text.content: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

rich_text.plain_text: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

text.content: "Adding 40 and 50 gives 90, which is too big. To find halfway, you need half of the difference. What's half of 10?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Adding 40 and 50 gives 90, which is too big. To find halfway, you need half of the difference. What's half of 10?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected guessing (lacks awareness of the halfway pattern strategy)

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected guessing (lacks awareness of the halfway pattern strategy)

text.content: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

rich_text.plain_text: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

text.content: "You don't need to guess. There's a pattern: halfway between any two lines is always 5 more than the lower line. Why is that?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You don't need to guess. There's a pattern: halfway between any two lines is always 5 more than the lower line. Why is that?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct strategy.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct strategy.

text.content: Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse. The space between 40 and 50 highlights briefly, then the halfway point at 45 glows.

rich_text.plain_text: Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse. The space between 40 and 50 highlights briefly, then the halfway point at 45 glows.

text.content: "Let me show you the pattern. The bar is halfway between 40 and 50. The lines are 10 apart. Half of 10 is 5. So halfway is always 5 more than the lower line: 40 plus 5 equals 45. That pattern works for any bar graph with scale of 10. Halfway between the lines is always 5 more."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you the pattern. The bar is halfway between 40 and 50. The lines are 10 apart. Half of 10 is 5. So halfway is always 5 more than the lower line: 40 plus 5 equals 45. That pattern works for any bar graph with scale of 10. Halfway between the lines is always 5 more."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next

text.content: "Knowing that pattern means you can read any bar graph with scale of 10, even values between the lines. That's a powerful strategy."

rich_text.plain_text: "Knowing that pattern means you can read any bar graph with scale of 10, even values between the lines. That's a powerful strategy."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next — toggle open to see current scene state

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next — toggle open to see current scene state

text.content: Vertical bar graph. Single bar highlighted at 45. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph. Single bar highlighted at 45. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

text.content: "You learned to read values at the lines and between the lines. You used 'half of 10 is 5' to figure out tricky values."

rich_text.plain_text: "You learned to read values at the lines and between the lines. You used 'half of 10 is 5' to figure out tricky values."

text.content: ⏭️ Student presses Next — toggle open to see current scene state

rich_text.plain_text: ⏭️ Student presses Next — toggle open to see current scene state

text.content: Vertical bar graph. Single bar highlighted at 45. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph. Single bar highlighted at 45. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

text.content: "That pattern-finding will help you when we look at all the scales together next time. You'll be able to pick the best one for any data."

rich_text.plain_text: "That pattern-finding will help you when we look at all the scales together next time. You'll be able to pick the best one for any data."
